title: pentaphylacaceae
intro: Pentaphylacaceae is a family of flowering plants that belongs to the order Ericales. This family consists of approximately 50 species of plants, primarily native to Asia, and is characterized by their simple leaves and white or purple flowers. The name Pentaphylacaceae is derived from the Greek words "pente," meaning five, and "phylakos," referring to the distinctive arrangement of the stamens in these plants. Pentaphylacaceae is a relatively small family of flowering plants, but it is of interest to botanists due to its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
